semanticCore: Meat, muscle, weave, battle, or close tie
quranicRelevance: Flesh/meat of animals/fish for eating (Lahm - 'lahman tariyyan'), and human flesh.
figurativeOrRareUsages: أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا (49:12): That he eats the flesh of his dead brother (graphic metaphor for backbiting/slandering).
لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا: Fresh meat (describing fresh seafood harvest from the sea).
summaryEn: The root refers to meat and flesh, contrasting the raw blessings of fresh seafood (Lahm) with the horrific metaphor of backbiting (eating a brother's flesh).
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى اللحم والبدن، مفصلاً النعمة الغذائية الطازجة لصيد البحر (لحماً طرياً) ومشبهاً الغيبة بأكل لحم الميت.
rawText: أَلْحَمَ خَرْقَهُ 4 [He closed up the hole thereof with a patch]; meaning a garment, or piece of cloth, and a skin, or hide. (TA in art. رقع.) === إِلْحَامُ الجِرَاحَاتِ [The consolidating of wounds]. (K in art. سبع.) ___ أَلْحَمَهُ عِرْضَهُ ‡ He empowered him to revile, vilify, or censure, him: (S, K, TA:) he made his honour, or reputation, to be to him [as] a 🡻لُحْمَة [or hawk's portion of the quarry]. (Har, p. 392.) === أَلْحِمْ مَا أَسْدَيْتَ: see أَسْدَى. المُلْتَحِمَةٌ [The tunica albuginea, or white of the eye: so in the present day]. (K, voce سَبَلٌ.) اِلْتَحَمَ 8 It coalesced, consolidated, closed up, or became closely united. (TA.) تَلَاحَمَ 6 It was joined, or knit, together. See K, voce مَزْفُورٌ. تَمْرٌ لَهُ لَحْمٌ [Dates having flesh]. (Msb in art. حشف.) === لَحْمٌ: see ثَرِيدٌ, last sentence. شَجَّةٌ مُتَلَاحِمَةٌ : see شَجَّةٌ, and بَازِلَةٌ (voce بَازِلٌ). شَحِمٌ لَحِمٌ : see مَحِضٌ and شَحِمٌ. لَحَّامٌ A butcher. (Fr, TA in art. سطر.) لَحْمَةٌ and 🡻لُحْمَةٌ The woof; or the threads that are woven into the سَدَى. or warp, of a piece of cloth. (Msb, &c.) لُحْمَةٌ : see 4, and لَحْمَةٌ. === لُحْمَةٌ شَابِكَةٌ: see مُشْتَبِكٌ. مَلَاحِمُ الفَرْجِ (K) The narrow, or strait, parts of the pudendum muliebre: (TA:) or rather, the fleshy parts thereof: the sing. مَلْحَمَةٌ signifying, accord. to analogy, a place of much flesh: see بِطَانٌ.
